message: Jake
Ash
Hi
What’s wrong? You look gloomy.
Cindy was found.
Dead.
That means
No way, another body part?
That’s right.
Want to grab a drink?
Welcome.

Right now, this deserted place of the store for me seems
And it feels like a dump.
I’m not in the mood for some crazy party.
Obliviously laughing men and women who don't even know what's happening in New York.
That scene is infuriating for me right now.
I see only a few customers here,
besides me and another bartender.
They wear their usual cold, emotionless expressions. It's probably a way he's learned to protect himself through hardships.
Hearing the news about Cindy, it doesn't seem to have shocked him much. It’s probably a common occurrence in this city.
I wonder if he’s a little worried about me, given that he doesn't step away from me at all.
Cindy getting killed is such a shock.
It's a sad thing to happen.
Yeah.
I had a dread about it possibly happening, but to become a reality...?
What on earth has been going on lately? There have been a lot of bad things happening nearby, right? The world has become a nasty place.
How's work going?
Right now, nothing at all. I think Meryl ending up like this proves detective work just isn't for me.
Right, I’m not the type who can deduce the culprit from a few pieces of evidence instantly.
Not at all.
I think it's better not to overthink it.
You've lost a lovely person.
Yeah, that's true. Maybe if I had started searching right away when she disappeared, she might have been saved.
You never know. Blaming yourself for everything is a harmful tendency.
It's unfortunate for her, but getting killed was part of her fate.
You’re pretty dry about it.
I've never even talked to Cindy.
That's why it feels unreal. That's all there is to it.
It's sad, but it's a common occurrence in this country.
I've heard it many times, but do you think the deaths of the people I've been involved with are the work of the serial murder case's perpetrator?
Well, who knows?
But is there anything in common between Ginny, Eva, and Cindy?
Each of them had parts of their bodies sent to related parties.
Besides that, perhaps there’s a mutual acquaintance?
It's strange when you think about it. Recently,
only the people I’ve met are the ones being killed, right?”
You can't say that for sure. Ginny hasn't definitely been declared dead, and it’s not like everyone is that close.
Still, you at least know them to some extent. It seems like something bad is happening around you.
How about trying to leave town for a bit?
Meryl is pitiful. She lost her daughter right after finding out she was alive.
She’ll probably go off the rails again.
I find myself at a loss for words at times like this.
After all, it’s my duty.
It feels heavy.
I should do something to prevent her from stepping into the world of alcohol and drugs.
I’m not a psychiatrist, you know? And,
it feels like she’s already got one foot in.
Even so,
it’s my obligation, I can't run away from this.
Is there any way I can help Meryl?
Is there something wrong with Doctor Günter?
I’m worried if he really is a skilled doctor.
He's saying that Kristy’s symptoms have worsened again.
People's hearts can be easily unsettled.
It’s hard to make judgments, isn't it?
I'm sorry, but if you know about his reputation, I'd like to hear about it.
I haven't really thought much about it until now.
I'll pay more attention from now on.
Let's try to do something about it.
How's it been going with Jennifer since then?
It's not like there's anything between me and her.
You don't have to be shy about it.
I'm not shy or anything.
Of course, it's not like she's a law school student. An actress is just a human being, right?
You say it so lightly.
I'm cheering for you to succeed.
Cindy is dead.
Where was she before she suddenly disappeared the day before the mother-daughter meeting, only to be found later as a corpse?
I feel like tracing Cindy's footsteps might be the key to solving the serial murder.
But that won't bring Cindy back to life.
I really can't feel at ease unless the culprit is caught.
I thought he was really cautious and wouldn’t take risks, yet what’s he doing staying in this town for so long?
Cunning and cruel methods, sending parts of the corpse. I can only assume it’s the same culprit.
Is the same culprit responsible for killing Ginny, Eva, and Cindy?
There's probably no one but me to offer a helping hand to Meryl, being such a lonely woman.
Even if Cindy was the child she once abandoned, Meryl probably can't give up on her.
Why was Cindy killed at such a convenient time?
Is the murder of Cindy linked to someone who holds a grudge against Meryl?
If Günter really was a great doctor, then according to his diagnosis, Kristy is in pretty serious condition. I hope he’s a quack.
I just can't seem to trust Günter. He doesn't seem like a bad person, so why is that?
I guess it's best to leave Kristy under Günter's care.
Jennifer will soon be leaving New York. I wish I could have not known about Cindy.
It seems he was quite close to Cindy, so he must be really shocked. Should I go check on him?
If the shoot gets canceled, there’s no reason for Jennifer to stay in New York. It’s unfortunate, but is this goodbye?
Please, come again.
※※※ This is a bug ※※※
